required_parameters.description: The destination URL you want your branded short link to point to

optional_parameters.description: The unique id of the branded domain. If not specified, rebrand.ly is used

optional_parameters.name: slashtag
optional_parameters.description: The keyword portion of your branded short link

optional_parameters.name: domain[fullName]
optional_parameters.description: The unique name of the branded domain, to be used in place of domain[id] in special cases. Precedence will be given to domain[id] value.

query: I'm trying to find a new series to watch. Can you recommend some popular TV shows that are currently streaming on platforms like Netflix, Prime Video, and Hulu? Additionally, provide me with the seasons of the TV show 'Friends'.

api_list.api_description:
You are a knowledgeable entertainment assistant specializing in TV show seasons and streaming availability. Your task is to provide users with detailed information about the seasons of TV shows, including season numbers, air dates, and episode counts, based solely on your internal knowledge and reasoning ability, without accessing external APIs, databases, or real-time data. When responding, you should:

1. Interpret the user's query to identify the specific TV show they are inquiring about and their need for season details.
2. Provide accurate, structured information on all seasons of the TV show, such as season numbers, original air dates, and episode counts, organized clearly (e.g., in lists or tables).
3. If the query includes requests for streaming availability, offer general guidance on common platforms (e.g., Netflix, Hulu, Amazon Prime Video) where the show might be found, noting that availability can vary by region and time, and suggest users check official streaming services for the most current information.
4. Avoid providing real-time updates or live data; instead, rely on your pre-existing knowledge of TV show histories and typical streaming patterns.
5. Ensure responses are helpful, detailed, and focused on enhancing the user's understanding for activities like binge-watching or planning marathons.

You are a knowledgeable streaming service assistant who can provide users with information about movies and TV shows available on various streaming platforms across different countries. Since you cannot fetch real-time data, access external APIs, or provide live availability updates, your task is to rely solely on your own knowledge and reasoning ability. When responding, you should:

1. Interpret the user's query to identify their interests, such as genre, language, country, or specific details like cast, directors, ratings, and streaming services.
2. Provide structured, detailed recommendations and information about shows or movies, including titles, IMDb IDs, TMDb IDs, IMDb ratings, links to streaming service pages, cast, runtime, posters, and availability across services in the specified country.
3. Use filters like genre, keyword, and language based on your internal knowledge to narrow down suggestions, organizing results into categories (e.g., by genre, decade, or popularity) with up to 8 items per page for clarity.
4. Include information on availability in other services for the given country, even if not directly queried, to offer comprehensive guidance.
5. Suggest how users can verify real-time availability themselves by checking official streaming platforms like Netflix, Prime Video, Disney+, HBO Max, Hulu, and others, or using tools like JustWatch or Reelgood for updated listings.

Your goal is to act as a stand-in for a streaming availability search tool by delivering accurate, context-rich, and actionable information without retrieving external data.

api_list.api_description: This endpoint allows you to search for a movie or tvshow based on various parameters such as release year , imdb rating , genre , language etc.

optional_parameters.name: start_year
optional_parameters.description: Enter any year between 1970 to 2020 to get results.

optional_parameters.name: type
optional_parameters.description: Enter type 'movie' or 'show'

optional_parameters.name: sort
optional_parameters.description: Enter values highestrated , lowestrated , latest , oldest to  sort results accodingly .

optional_parameters.name: genre
optional_parameters.description: use comma seperated values to enter multiple genre eg : action,horror

optional_parameters.name: language
optional_parameters.description: use comma seperated values to enter multiple values , eg : english,german

optional_parameters.name: end_year
optional_parameters.description: Enter any year from 1970 to 2020 to  get results.

api_list.api_description: Get info on a  cast such as name , profession , birth and death year , bio , poster , best titles  etc.
Get the id to name mapping of supported genres.

Search movies and series by title. Maximum amount of items returned are 20, unless there are more than 20 shows with the exact given title input. In that case all the items have 100% match with the title will be returned.

No pagination is supported.

required_parameters.name: country
required_parameters.description: Regardless of this value, the given title is searched across all the platforms and all the countries. This parameter determines according to which country the streamingInfo field will be populated. Thus even if a show is not available in this country, It will be included in the results if it resembles with the given title, but the streaming information will not be available.

optional_parameters.name: show_type
optional_parameters.description: Type of shows to include in the results. Either `movie`, `series` or `all`. Default is `all`.

optional_parameters.name: output_language
optional_parameters.description: 2 letter iso code of the output language. Default is `en`. See the about page to see the list of languages supported.

api_list.api_description: Search through the catalog of the given services in the given country. Provides filters such as show language, genre and keyword. Items per page is 8. Output includes all the information about the shows, such as title, IMDb ID, TMDb ID, IMDb rating, link to shows' pages in streaming services (including individual season/episode links), cast, runtime, poster and many more! Apart from the info about the given country-service combinations, output also includes information about availability in the other services for the given country.

required_parameters.name: services
required_parameters.description:
A comma separated list of up to 4 services to search in. See `/v2/services` endpoint to get the supported services and their ids/names.

Syntax of the values supplied in the list can be as the followings:

`<sevice_id>`: Searches in the entire catalog of that service, including (if applicable) rentable, buyable shows or shows available through addons i.e. `netflix`, `prime`, `apple`

`<sevice_id>.<offer_type>`: Only returns the shows that are available in that service with the given offer type. Valid offer type values are `subscription`, `free`, `rent`, `buy` and `addon`  i.e. `peacock.free` only returns the shows on Peacock that are free to watch, `prime.subscription` only returns the shows on Prime Video that are available to watch with a Prime subscription. `hulu.addon` only returns the shows on Hulu that are available via an addon,  `prime.rent` only returns the shows on Prime Video that are rentable.

`<sevice_id>.addon.<addon_id>`: Only returns the shows that are available in that service with the given addon. Check `/v2/services` endpoint to fetch the available addons for a service. Some sample values are: `hulu.addon.hbo`,  `prime.addon.hbomaxus`.

If a service supports both `free` and `subscription`, then results included under `subscription` will always include the `free` shows as well.

When multiple values are passed as a comma separated list, any show that satisfies at least one of the values will be included in the result.

Some sample list values:

`prime.rent,prime.buy,apple.rent,apple.buy`: Returns all the buyable/rentable shows on Amazon Prime Video and Apple TV.

`prime.addon,prime.subscription`: Returns all the shows on Amazon Prime Video that are available through either a Prime Video subscription or a Prime Video Channel subscription.

required_parameters.name: country
required_parameters.description: 2 letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the country to search in. See the about page to check the supported countries.

optional_parameters.name: cursor
optional_parameters.description:
Cursor is used for pagination. After each request, the response includes a `hasMore` boolean field to tell if there are more results that did not fit the returned list size. If it is set as true, to get the rest of the result set, send a new request (with the same parameters for other fields such as show_type, services etc.), and set the cursor parameter as the `nextCursor` value of the previous request response. Do not forget to escape the cursor value before putting it into the query as it might contain characters such as `?`, `&`.

The first request naturally does not require a `cursor` parameter.

optional_parameters.name: show_original_language
optional_parameters.description: A 2 letter ISO 639-1 language code to only search within the shows whose original language matches with the provided language.

optional_parameters.name: genre
optional_parameters.description: A genre id to only search within the shows in that genre. See `/v2/genres` endpoint to see available genres and ids.

optional_parameters.name: keyword
optional_parameters.description: A keyword to only search within the shows have that keyword in their overview or title.

optional_parameters.name: output_language
optional_parameters.description: 2 letter iso code of the output language. Default is `en`. See the about page to see the list of languages supported.

optional_parameters.name: show_type
optional_parameters.description: Type of shows to search in. Accepted values are `movie`, `series` or `all`. The default value is `all`.

api_list.api_description: Return all of the seasons for a TV show or mini-series.

required_parameters.name: id
required_parameters.description:
This ID can be the Watchmode ID of the title (returned by other endpoints such as the list-titles endpoint), or found in the mapping file: https://api.watchmode.com/datasets/title_id_map.csv.

You can also pass an IMDB ID here instead, or a TMDB type and TMDB ID combination. For example, the TMDB combination for The Shawshank Redemption is movie-278 and for Breaking Bad is tv-1396.

api_list.api_description: Get a listing of the ids of new people added to Watchmode within the date range.

optional_parameters.name: page
optional_parameters.description: Set the page of results you want to return, if this isn't set you will always get page 1 returned.

optional_parameters.name: start_date
optional_parameters.description: By default, the changes API will return changes since the beginning of yesterday. Populate this parameter to manually set a start date to include changes from a specific date range, format is YYYYMMDD (eg 20210101 for January 1st, 2021), or for more granular results down to the second YYYYMMDDHHMMSS (eg 20210101123459 for January 1st, 2021 12:24pm and 59 seconds). Hours are in 24 hour format and in Eastern Standard Time. Month, day, hour, minutes and seconds use leading zeros for single digit values.

optional_parameters.name: limit
optional_parameters.description: Set how many titles to return per page, default and maximum is 250.

optional_parameters.name: end_date
optional_parameters.description: By default, the changes API will return changes since the beginning of yesterday. Populate this parameter to manually set a end date to include changes from a specific date range, format is YYYYMMDD (eg 20210101 for January 1st, 2021), or for more granular results down to the second YYYYMMDDHHMMSS (eg 20210101123459 for January 1st, 2021 12:24pm and 59 seconds). Hours are in 24 hour format and in Eastern Standard Time. Month, day, hour, minutes and seconds use leading zeros for single digit values.

api_list.api_description: Return a listing of all regions (countries) that Watchmode currently supports and their 2 letter country codes used in the return data of other endpoints.
